title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Automation and Data Client Systems · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A compliance analyst reconstructing a partial ingestion handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for deterministic tests, fault injection, contract evidence, and observability, producing fault-injected client verification harness. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers fault-injected client verification harness after a delayed changed case. Learne…
objectives: Test request construction and response handling with an injected deterministic transport and sanitized fixtures. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Use a local test server for protocol integration while controlling redirects, delays, truncation, TLS, and cleanup. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Verify documented provider and consumer contracts against changed schemas, status codes, and version boundaries. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Http Telemetry
steps.instruction:
review case — compliance handoff automation test P-5 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Assert the outgoing request, cleanup, failure taxonomy, attempt count, low-cardinality telemetry, and no secret leakage. Competency probe: Record stable client duration, method, route or safe target, status, error type, attempts, bytes, and trace context without high-cardinality secrets. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
